channel.title: clawfeedradar
channel.description: Personal feed radar powered by clawsqlite.

item.title: Moon-bound Artemis II astronauts enjoy a relaxed day in space
item.description: The Artemis II astronauts continued their long coast to the moon, capturing stunning photos along the way.

item.title: Why March jobs report far exceeded expectations
item.description: March was a good month for U.S. job growth, according to the latest report that blew away Wall Street expectations with 178,000 jobs added. CBS News senior business and tech correspondent Jo Ling Kent has more.

item.title: Farm bankruptcies increase over last five years, Politico analysis finds
item.description: U.S. farmers are struggling to stay afloat as the cost to operate keeps increasing. According to new Agriculture Department data analyzed by Politico, the U.S. has lost nearly 150,000 farms in the last five years. Rachel Shin, one of the authors of that analysis, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: U.S. still searching for downed fighter jet crew member as Iran war rages
item.description: As the Iran war enters a sixth week, a search-and-rescue mission for a downed fighter jet crew member is ongoing. The pilot was recovered.

item.title: What downed U.S. fighter jet signifies about overall war
item.description: A search and rescue mission is underway after Iran shot down an American F-15 fighter jet. The aircraft had two crew members on board- U.S. forces rescued one, and the other is still missing. CBS News national security contributor Sam Vinograd has more.

item.title: FBI arrests suspected Unabomber in Montana (1996)
item.description: On April 3, 1996, the FBI arrested Theodore Kaczynski in the Unabomber case, ending one of the longest and most intense manhunts in U.S. history. Watch CBS News' coverage from that day.

item.title: Previewing NCAA Final Four games
item.description: The NCAA Men's Final Four tips off on Saturday night. The first game features number three Illinois colliding with powerhouse University of Connecticut. Then, there will be a battle between the two No.1 seeds, Michigan and Arizona. Eddie Pells, national sports writer for the Associated Press, joins "The Daily Report" to preview the games.

item.title: A school learned sign language to communicate with its only deaf student
item.description: New Hampshire is one of the few states in the nation that doesn't have a dedicated school for the deaf.

item.title: Trump orders DHS to pay all employees despite shutdown
item.description: President Trump ordered the Department of Homeland Security to find a way to pay "each and every employee" of the agency.

item.title: A deaf student felt alone until his school stepped up in a special way
item.description: Seven-year-old Ben O'Reilly is deaf and has other special needs. His aide said he felt isolated at school until an act of kindness from his fellow classmates started a wave. Steve Hartman has the story in "On the Road."

item.title: NASA shares first photos of Earth taken by Artemis II
item.description: The photo shows the entire planet, as well as the Northern and Southern lights.

item.title: Former CIA Director David Petraeus lauds Ukraine's "extraordinary innovation" in war with Russia
item.description: Former CIA Director David Petraeus spoke at the Kyiv Security Forum on Friday, praising Ukraine's military and saying it has redefined modern warfare. Petraeus sat down with CBS News international reporter Aidan Stretch to discuss further.
